name: Drawer
description: This doc.json consolidates the two old design-system/components docs — NavigationDrawer and SideSheet — which were both confirmed to duplicate-map to one real component: MUI's Drawer. A Drawer is a surface anchored to an edge of the viewport. Docked (variant="permanent"/"persistent") it reads as navigation or a persistent side panel that reflows the page; as an overlay (variant="temporary", the MUI default) it reads as a modal side sheet — contextual detail, a filters panel, or a mobile nav overlay — sitting over a backdrop that traps focus. Both use cases are the same component with a different `variant`/`anchor`, not two components.
keyRule: Pick `variant` by whether the page should reflow around the drawer ("permanent"/"persistent", docked) or the drawer should sit over the page and block it until dismissed ("temporary", overlay). Content is entirely your own children — Drawer has no built-in nav-items/header/footer props; compose List/ListItemButton for navigation, or a header Box + form/content + footer actions for a side sheet.

variants.name: Permanent (docked navigation drawer)
variants.whenToUse: Default desktop primary navigation — always visible alongside content. variant="permanent". NOTE: fleet-web's real primary nav is a bespoke MainSideNavbar, not this Drawer — this variant documents a real, supported API shape for building nav-drawer UIs, it isn't currently how fleet-web's own main nav is implemented.

variants.name: Persistent (toggleable docked drawer)
variants.whenToUse: A docked drawer the operator can show/hide without it overlaying content when open. variant="persistent".

variants.name: Temporary (modal side sheet / overlay)
variants.whenToUse: Contextual detail beside a list (e.g. a selected vehicle's summary), an editing flow that should hold focus, or a mobile nav overlay. variant="temporary" (the MUI default) — renders over a backdrop and traps focus like a Dialog.

variants.name: Filters panel
variants.whenToUse: Hosts the advanced filters for a DataGrid — typically variant="temporary" anchored right.

anatomy.name: Paper
anatomy.description: the drawer surface itself — MUI's real `paper` slot; holds whatever children you render.

anatomy.name: Backdrop
anatomy.description: the dimmed scrim behind a temporary-variant drawer — MUI's real `backdrop` slot; not rendered for permanent/persistent.

anatomy.name: Your header content
anatomy.description: not a built-in prop — compose a Box/Toolbar with a title + close IconButton as the first child if you need one (mirrors the old SideSheet's Header anatomy).

anatomy.name: Your nav/content
anatomy.description: for navigation use, a List of ListItemButton items (mirrors the old NavigationDrawer's Item/Section anatomy); for a side sheet, arbitrary detail, tabs, or a form.

anatomy.name: Your footer content
anatomy.description: not a built-in prop — compose your own action row as the last child if the sheet's content needs footer actions.

behaviors.name: Docked vs overlay
behaviors.description: Permanent/persistent variants coexist with the page and reflow content around them (you're responsible for the layout reflow — Drawer doesn't do it for you); temporary sits over a backdrop and blocks the page until dismissed.

behaviors.name: Selection (navigation use)
behaviors.description: If using Drawer for navigation, the current destination is a `selected` ListItemButton; selecting an item navigates and updates it — this is List/ListItemButton behavior, not Drawer's.

behaviors.name: Open with context (side-sheet use)
behaviors.description: Selecting a record opens a temporary-variant Drawer with its detail; keep the underlying list visible and highlight the selection — an app-level pattern, not built into Drawer.

behaviors.name: Dismiss
behaviors.description: onClose fires on Escape or a backdrop click (temporary variant only, via the underlying Modal); permanent/persistent variants have no backdrop/Escape dismissal — you provide your own close control.

behaviors.name: Entrance animation
behaviors.description: The karoo-ui wrapper forces slotProps.transition.appear=true, so the Slide transition always animates in, even on first mount.

states.treatment: Medium-emphasis icon + label on a ListItemButton.
Primary-tint background, primary-dark label/icon — pass `selected` to ListItemButton.
Paper over backdrop; focus trapped inside via the underlying Modal.
Docked; page content reflows to make room (your layout's responsibility).

specs.value: No default — Drawer's Paper has no built-in width; set it via sx/PaperProps (e.g. ~240–256px for a nav drawer, ~320–400px for a side sheet, per prior usage)

Real default `elevation` prop is 16, applied to the Paper — most visible on temporary/modal variants; docked variants typically read flush, bordered by semantic.color.border.default instead of a shadow
4px (semantic.radius.default) on the Paper, via theme.shape — not commonly used since drawers are usually edge-to-edge/full-height
Roboto 16–20px / 500 is a reasonable choice (semantic.typography.scale.titleMedium or .h6) — not enforced by Drawer itself

props.name: open
props.description: Narrowed from MUI's real `boolean` by the karoo-ui wrapper — app convention: a Drawer is only ever rendered while open; unmount it to close instead of passing open={false}.

props.name: variant
props.description: 'permanent'/'persistent' = docked (reflows content, no backdrop). 'temporary' (default) = overlay with backdrop and focus trap, like a modal side sheet.

props.name: anchor
props.description: Edge the drawer appears from. Default 'left' — a nav drawer is typically 'left', a side sheet typically 'right'.

props.name: children
props.description: Arbitrary content — a List for navigation, or a header/content/footer composition for a side sheet. There are no built-in sections/navItems/title/footer props.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called on Escape or a backdrop click — only fires for variant="temporary" (routed through ModalProps to the underlying Modal).

props.name: ModalProps
props.description: Props applied to the underlying Modal when variant="temporary" (e.g. to customize backdrop behavior).

props.name: sx
props.description: Standard MUI system prop — typically used to set the Paper's width/height since Drawer has no width prop of its own.

doThis: Pick variant by reflow-vs-overlay intent, not by copying a specific old NavigationDrawer/SideSheet prop shape.
For navigation use: group items by purpose in a List, keep the top level short, show one clear active (selected) destination, hide items the operator can't access.
For side-sheet use: keep the underlying list/context visible when possible, and give the sheet a clear close control.
Set the Paper's width explicitly via sx — there is no default width.
dontDoThis: Don't make a navigation-use Drawer a flat dump of every feature — group by purpose.
Don't stuff a full multi-step task into a narrow temporary-variant drawer — use a page or Dialog instead.
Don't block the page with a temporary/modal Drawer for read-only detail if a persistent/docked one would keep context better.
Don't expect backdropClick/Escape to fire onClose on permanent/persistent variants — there's no backdrop; provide your own close affordance.
accessibility: For variant="temporary": MUI's underlying Modal supplies role=dialog/aria-modal, traps focus, and restores it to the trigger on close, same as Dialog.
For variant="permanent"/"persistent": wrap navigation content in a <nav> landmark yourself (e.g. component="nav" or a nav-role List) — Drawer doesn't add this for you.
The active navigation item should expose aria-current (via your own ListItemButton).
Any close control you compose needs its own accessible name.
Escape closes temporary/modal drawers by default; permanent/persistent drawers have no Escape/backdrop dismissal by design.

related.name: Dialog
related.why: Blocking decisions that demand full attention, vs. a Drawer's context-preserving overlay

related.name: List
related.why: The typical children for navigation-style Drawer content

related.name: Menu
related.why: A lighter-weight, smaller temporary surface for a short list of choices
